title: RFC 9309 Matching, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Go Adapter · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for rfc 9309 matching, failure semantics, caching, and go adapter and accepts only compile and runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, parser, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for conformance-tested robots decision engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Fetch `/robots.txt` at the correct service origin with an honest stable product token and contact identity.
Apply RFC 9309 product-token matching, group merge, percent encoding, longest rule, and allow tie behavior.
Classify success, redirects, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, stale cache, and owner-stop states conservatively.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

stimulus.title: Robots Extension Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat sitemap, crawl-delay, request-rate, and other extensions as separately documented hints or owner policy.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat sitemap, crawl-delay, request-rate, and other extensions as separately documented hints or owner policy.
